chunks.text: Artificial intelligence, that is, AI, has made remarkable progress over the past ten years.
Algorithms that learn from vast amounts of data now support humans in many fields
Diagnostic assistance, self - driving cars, and natural language processing.
Each application is changing our lives.
In some industry, automation has already transformed the landscape of labor.
But rapid technological progress comes with serious risk as well.
Invasion of privacy, the loss of jobs, the spread of misinformation.
Our society must face these consequence head on.
Who bears the responsibility for an algorithm?
When a self - driving car causes an accident, who is held accountable in court?
These questions still do not have complete answers.
Governments around the world have begun to build regulation for AI.
The European Union has already established a comprehensive law concerning the ethical use of AI.
Whether it is used for an ethical purpose or not depends on our human judgment.
AI must exist not so that it can think in our place
but so that we can think better than before.
